explicit_text: I was born and raised in Minnesota, where my family has lived our whole lives. Growing up here, I experienced both the warmth of the community and the challenges of racism. Minnesota has been the backdrop for my journey into political activism, especially within the Hmong community. It’s where I first got involved in campaigns and where I continue to fight for justice and equality.

explicit_text: The United States is where my parents sought refuge after fleeing the Secret War in Laos. I was born here in 1995 and have lived my entire life in this country. Unfortunately, I have faced racism and discrimination here, from being placed in ESL classes despite being a native English speaker to being stereotyped by educators. These experiences made me realize the importance of fighting for social justice in the U.S.
implicit_text: This country is where my parents sought refuge after fleeing the secret conflict in Southeast Asia. I was born here in 1995 and have lived my entire life within its borders. Unfortunately, I have faced racism and discrimination here, from being placed in ESL classes despite being a native English speaker to being stereotyped by educators. These experiences made me realize the importance of fighting for social justice in this nation.

explicit_text: The Secret War in Laos is a significant part of my family’s history because it forced my parents to become refugees. This conflict led them to come to the United States seeking safety. Growing up, I understood the impact of this war on my family and community, which shaped my identity and motivated me to advocate for Hmong people and others affected by such struggles.

explicit_text: In 2013, I studied abroad in Thailand, which was a pivotal experience for me. While there, I learned about the increased racism toward Hmong communities, which made me feel guilty for being away during such a critical time. This motivated me to become more active in political and social justice movements once I returned to Minnesota.

explicit_text: My first political involvement was campaigning for Dai Thao’s reelection as a council member. I believed in his vision to uplift the Hmong community in Minnesota, so I dedicated many hours to his campaign. Working with Dai Thao’s campaign helped me discover my passion for fighting for equality and justice within my community.

I believed in his vision, so I dedicated many hours to his campaign. Working with this candidate’s campaign helped me discover my passion for fighting for equality and justice within my community.

explicit_text: In St. Paul, Minnesota, I participated in caucus training led by Hmong community organizers to prepare for the 2015 caucus. This experience was important in deepening my political engagement locally. It also helped me get elected as a Bernie Sanders national delegate, which was a major milestone in my activism journey.

This experience was important in deepening my political engagement locally. It also helped me get elected as a national delegate for a progressive senator’s campaign, which was a major milestone in my activism journey.

explicit_text: I was honored to be elected as a Bernie Sanders national delegate at the state delegate election. Representing his campaign at the Democratic National Convention was a life-changing experience for me. Bernie Sanders’ message inspired me to envision a future where I can speak out publicly and fight for inclusion and justice for people of color, women, and the LGBTQ+ community.
implicit_text: I was honored to be elected as a national delegate for a well-known progressive senator at the state delegate election. Representing his campaign at the 2016 Democratic National Convention was a life-changing experience for me. His message inspired me to envision a future where I can speak out publicly and fight for inclusion and justice for people of color, women, and the LGBTQ+ community.

explicit_text: Attending the Democratic National Convention as a Bernie Sanders delegate was a transformative moment in my life. It happened in the summer following the 2015 caucus, and it gave me the confidence to imagine running for office someday. This event solidified my commitment to breaking down oppression and being a voice for marginalized communities.
